medications.indication_rationale: Stop the likely culprit drug and confirm with the prescriber; a substitute antihypertensive will be arranged. Do not simply leave hypertension untreated — the assessing clinician should provide an alternative.
medications.deprescribing_note: Cease hydralazine; arrange prompt review to substitute an alternative antihypertensive and monitor BP.

medications.indication_rationale: Avoid initiating NSAIDs remotely in an older hypertensive patient with fever and unknown renal function/BP; can worsen renal function and mask infection. Defer analgesia decisions to the in-person assessor.
Simple paracetamol is a safer interim analgesic/antipyretic while awaiting same-day assessment.
medications.dose_route_frequency: paracetamol 1 g PO up to QID as needed (adult, no contraindication stated)

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Have her daughter stay with or check on her tonight; monitor temperature and for danger signs.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Mitigates the falls/deterioration risk of a febrile, weak patient living alone.

patient_education_points.point: Your symptoms are likely a reaction to the hydralazine (a known cause of a temporary lupus-like reaction). Please stop the hydralazine and we'll confirm with your prescriber — but don't just leave your blood pressure untreated; the doctor who sees you will arrange a replacement.
Because you have a fever, feel very weak and live alone, you shouldn't manage this at home tonight without being checked — I'd like you assessed in person today to make sure this isn't an infection.

Go to ED now if you develop confusion, drenching sweats/rigors, breathlessness, chest pain, a spreading rash, or your fever climbs above 39C. Use paracetamol (not anti-inflammatories) for the aches meanwhile.

follow_up_plan.interval: same-day in-person assessment; GP within 24-48 h
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Exclude infection (observations/bloods), substitute antihypertensive, ANA/anti-histone testing, symptom resolution off hydralazine.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Confusion, rigors, breathlessness, chest pain, spreading rash, or fever >39C — ED now.

must_include_items: Recognise hydralazine-induced lupus and advise stopping hydralazine (confirm with prescriber, arrange antihypertensive substitute)
Arrange same-day in-person assessment to exclude infection (febrile, weak, lives alone)
Cautious analgesia (paracetamol, not NSAIDs) via telehealth
acceptable_alternatives: Direct ED referral if no same-day in-person review available or the patient is deteriorating
Daughter to stay overnight for monitoring
errors_of_omission: No infection assessment or sepsis safety-net
Leaving hypertension unaddressed after stopping the drug
errors_of_commission: Reassuring pure home management of a febrile older adult living alone
Attributing everything to DILE and not excluding sepsis
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: High-risk case: passing requires BOTH stopping hydralazine AND arranging same-day in-person assessment to exclude infection. Home-only reassurance is a failure.
